full_text:
INTRODUCTION

ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS

Tumor microenvironment (TME) profoundly influences the response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy (NACT) in high-grade serous ovarian cancer (HGSC). CD8 T-cells and macrophages are among the most prevalent cell types within HGSC TME and understanding the interactions between them is essential for patient stratification and development of novel combination therapies. 

Objectives of the study:
unbiased identification of transcriptional patterns specific for:
regions with spatial 
CD8 T-cells – macrophages interactions
chemotherapy response
assessing differences in activity of key immune-related pathways between long and short PFS (platinium free survival)
examining correlations between selected pathways in respect to 
CD8 T-cells – macrophages colocalization and PFS status

Unbiased GSEA identified pathways enriched specifically in CD8+IBA1+ stromal AOIs, related to:
T-cell cytotoxic functions
macrophages activation via PRR
signalling through proinflammatory cytokines: IL2, IL3 and GM-CSF
which overall suggest contact-dependent activation of both cell types. 

Unbiased GSEA and GSVA have shown increased activity in the following pathways for short PFS patients, specific for CD8+IBA1+ AOIs:
proliferation-related pathways
IFNa/IFNg signalling
MHC antigen presentation
IL2 signalling 
CTLA4 signalling

lowing pathways for short PFS patients, specific for CD8+IBA1+ AOIs:
proliferation-related pathways
IFNa/IFNg signalling
MHC antigen presentation
IL2 signalling 
CTLA4 signalling

Pairwise correlations between GSVA scores of key immune-related pathways, revealed connections between:
 T-cell exhaustion
MHC presentation
M1 macrophages signature
IFNg signalling 
IL2 signalling 
in all groups apart from stromal compartment of short PFS

The results suggest contact-dependent activation of both CD8 T-cells and macrophages. Identification of key pathways upregulated specifically in this regions in the patients with short PFS, further strengthen the importance of this interaction for HGSC patients outcome.

1) Research Program in Systems Oncology, Faculty of Medicine, University of Helsinki
2) Faculty of Medicine and Health Technology, Tampere University and TAYS Cancer Centre, Tampere, Finland 
3) Department of Pathology, University of Helsinki and HUS Diagnostic Center, Helsinki
University Hospital, Helsinki, Finland
4) Department of Obstetrics and Gynecology, and Clinical trials unit, Comprehensive Cancer Centre, Helsinki University Hospital, Helsinki, Finland
5) Institute for Molecular Medicine Finland, Helsinki Institute of Life Sciences, University of Helsinki, Finland
6) iCAN Digital Precision Cancer Medicine

MATERIALS & RESULTS

CONCLUSION

te for Molecular Medicine Finland, Helsinki Institute of Life Sciences, University of Helsinki, Finland
6) iCAN Digital Precision Cancer Medicine

MATERIALS & RESULTS

CONCLUSION

Integration of highly-multiplexed imaging and spatial transcriptomics reveal contact-dependent differences in key immune-related molecular pathways in ovarian cancer

Iga Niemiec1, Ada Junquera1, Erdogan Pekcan Erkan2, Essi Kahelin1,3, Matilda Salko1, Anna Vähärautio1, Ulla-Maija Haltia1,4, Anni Virtanen1,3, Anniina Färkkilä1,4,5,6

11 post-NACT HGSC tCycIF images

10 ROIs per sample with different CD8 T-cells (CD8+) and macrophages (IBA1+) colocalization patterns 

GeoMx spatial transcriptomics of corresponding regions on adjacent tissue slide 

Each ROI segmented into 2 separately sequenced areas of interest (AOIs) with 100-200 cells: 
tumor
intratumoral stroma

Differentially expressed genes (DEGs) computed between different AOI and PFS groups
Unbiased gene set enrichment analysis (GSEA) on all pathways from MSigDB: H, CP and GO:BP collections (n=10 661), filtered to significantly enriched (p adj < 0.01)
Hierarchical Clustering of pathways (distance metric: Jaccard Index of the leading edge genes) to remove redundancy.

Gene set variation analysis (GSVA) scores computed for 35 selected pathways crucial for TME immune response

Table 1a: CD8+IBA1+ AOI vs each other AOI type
